chunks.text: CDSS CS Commencement - EECS at Berkeley CDSS CS Commencement CDSS Commencement 2024 (Photo / Kayla Sim / UC Berkeley College of Computing, Data Science, and Society) 2026 Commencement The College of Computing, Data Science, and Society (CDSS) Undergr

omputing, Data Science, and Society (CDSS) Undergraduate Commencement Ceremonies on May 22, 2026, will celebrate the graduating class of the Computer Science, Data Science, and Statistics programs. Two ceremonies will be held to accommodate graduates

o ceremonies will be held to accommodate graduates and their guests: 9:00 am PT 2:00 pm PT Registration Registration for graduating CDSS students opens in early February. See the CDSS commencement website for more details. Contact cs-advising@berkele

site for more details. Contact cs-advising@berkeley.edu if you have any questions. Ceremonies & Convocations There are three ceremonies held to honor those completing their Bachelor’s degree during the current academic year. Attendance and participat

e current academic year. Attendance and participation in any of these events are voluntary. The university-wide commencement ceremony is an annual all-campus event where graduating seniors from all academic disciplines are given a time to come togeth

ademic disciplines are given a time to come together to celebrate their achievements at UC Berkeley. No names are called, and diplomas are not distributed. Details can be found here. College graduation ceremonies are held beginning in mid-May —full l

n ceremonies are held beginning in mid-May —full list of all ceremonies. Information for all commencement ceremonies Parking During commencement, visitors may park in designated daytime campus parking lots on a first-come, first-served basis, for $10

lots on a first-come, first-served basis, for $10. The closest campus parking lots to the Greek Theatre are the Foothill Lot (Hearst at Highland Ave.) and Lower Hearst Structure – Level 2 (enter from Scenic Ave.). Please read parking signs carefully

Scenic Ave.). Please read parking signs carefully. The Maxwell Garage, a public parking lot on the corner of Stadium Rim Way and Gayley Road, will be selling commencement parking for $25. Parking is also available in the surrounding city public par

also available in the surrounding city public parking lots on the south and west sides of campus; rates and hours vary. Disabled Parking: All designated disabled spaces and non-reserved spaces will be available at $10 to disabled patrons with certif

e available at $10 to disabled patrons with certified disabled placards or license plates on a first-come, first-served basis. For guests who would like a shorter distance from their car to the entrance, it is possible for a car to pull up temporaril

ce, it is possible for a car to pull up temporarily in the Bowles parking lot and drop off guests at the south gate. For more information about parking, see the Parking and Transportation website for commencement visitor parking locations for daytime

commencement visitor parking locations for daytime commencement ceremonies at the Greek Theatre. Due to the extreme limitations of on campus and surrounding area parking, we advise guests to use public transportation. Graduation Announcements and App

c transportation. Graduation Announcements and Apparel The Cal student store sells announcements, invitations, caps, tassels, honor cords, gowns, etc. which you can either purchase as a package (Grad Packs) or individually. The ASUC takes orders as e

Packs) or individually. The ASUC takes orders as early as March. Cost is approximately $45.00 for Bachelor’s gown, cap, and tassel. For further information, go to the Graduation section of the Cal Student Store website , or contact the Special Servic

dent Store website , or contact the Special Services Department of ASUC, 510-642-9000, ext. 680/681. Pictures of the Ceremony GradImages will be photographing the ceremony. In the week following the ceremony, proof order forms will be mailed to all g

eremony, proof order forms will be mailed to all graduates for viewing. You will also be able to view their proofs and place orders by visiting the GradImages website. Degree List vs. Commencement List The degree list is how you officially signal to

t The degree list is how you officially signal to the university that you are ready to graduate and are in the process of completing your final courses for your degree. To graduate, you must add yourself to the degree list by the beginning of the sem

elf to the degree list by the beginning of the semester (the spring, summer, or fall) you expect to complete all of your graduation requirements (e.g., breadth, double major, minor). This is automatically done through Cal Central during your final se

ally done through Cal Central during your final semester (or by CDSS petition, once the semester begins). The commencement list is the names of all registered graduates who will go into the printed program for the May commencement ceremony. Candidate

ogram for the May commencement ceremony. Candidates with graduation dates or expected graduate dates below may participate in the CDSS CS Commencement: Fall 2025 Spring, Summer, and Fall 2026 Verification of Your Degree Official Transcript: Grades fr

tion of Your Degree Official Transcript: Grades from your final semester appear three to four weeks after the last day of final exams. The date of your degree will appear on your transcript approximately three months after the term in which the degre

ely three months after the term in which the degree was conferred. Information about the availability of transcripts showing the degree and how to order transcripts and diplomas. Certificate of Completion: If you need proof that you have completed al

tion: If you need proof that you have completed all of your requirements for the degree before your final transcript is available, you can obtain a Certificate of Completion from the College of Computing, Data Science and Society , at cdss-undergrad@

ing, Data Science and Society , at cdss-undergrad@berkeley.edu . This certificate is available only after all grades from your final semester have been posted to your transcript . Letter of Intent : If you expect to graduate at the end of the current

f you expect to graduate at the end of the current term and need to provide verification to a third party, you may request a Letter of Intent to Graduate from the College of Computing, Data Science and Society, at cdss-undergrad@berkeley.edu . You mu

d Society, at cdss-undergrad@berkeley.edu . You must bring with you a Completion of Major memo which you can get from the CS Advising Office, at cs-advising@cs.berkeley.edu . The Letter of Intent will be generated within 10 working days. Visitor Info

be generated within 10 working days. Visitor Information We hope that you will find most of the information you need on dining and accommodations for Berkeley and the surrounding area at the following websites: Berkeley Convention and Visitors Burea
